solutions.solution: Q. 17-21: The logic is this: the words get arranged in interchanging places. That is, what occupies the first place goes to the last place, the second word goes to the first place and so on. Input: this west to prepare the domestic industry Step I: domestic industry to prepare the this west Step II: domestic industry to prepare the this west Step III: domestic industry prepare to the this west

Q. 22-26: Study the changes very carefully. What happens? Clearly, it is a case of simple arrangement in ascending or descending order. Look at the way 31 and 17 are brought together, so also 33 and 22. Multiple of 11 and then multiples of 11. So the clue: something to do with multiples. What really happens is the following: in case of a composite number, the largest factor (other than the number itself) is taken into account. In the case of a prime number, the number itself is considered. In case there are two multiples, the larger multiple is arranged first. Thus 37 = 37 × 1, 29 = 29 × 1, 17 = 17 × 1, 51 = 17 × 3, 46 = 23 × 2. 22 = 11 × 2, 71 = 71 × 1, 33 = 11 × 3 etc. Now, the underlined parts are what numbers and these are arranged in a descending order. That is why 71 comes after 29 because the key number for 46 is 23, which is less than 29. Also, when a number is arranged, it interchanges its position with the wrongly-placed number.
